claim_to_evidence_map.claim: Time-varying susceptibility field reproducible from public CMIP6 + GLDAS + SoilGrids + NCEER inputs
Climate shift signal under SSP5-8.5 2100 statistically distinguishable from sham control
evidence_files: ai_autoboost/outputs/round1/statistical_tests.csv (Wilcoxon z=5.88, p_Bonf≈0; Control-NoCC z=0.75, p=0.45 ✓ sham)

claim_to_evidence_map.claim: CG-STG vs static framework gain at 2100 SSP5-8.5
Static-bias spatially structured (deltaic > coastal > others, with arid + high-altitude null-or-weakly-negative)
R2's specific Mexico-City significantly-negative finding was a 10-seed type-I error
claim_to_evidence_map.note: R2 grade A finding downgraded to R3 grade C; documented as scientific self-correction

claim_to_evidence_map.claim: Climate → soil → liquefaction → damage mediator chain identifiable by PCMCI
Cohort regression on inverse-GW depth explains substantial variance of climate gap
Cascading regimes differ by lifeline class (water > power, transport > building)
GraphSAGE cascading surrogate reduces LOCO RMSE vs MLP baseline
Cohort regression robust under input perturbation
Pipeline reproducible bit-level on fixed seed
Cold-region permafrost is a distinct mediator (DEPRECATED)
claim_to_evidence_map.note: Originally proposed in R0 Brief; never met evidence threshold; explicitly deprecated in LIMITATIONS

claim_to_evidence_map.claim: Deltaic > inland (> arid) in climate gap
Differentiation from Wang et al. Nat. Commun. 2025 explicit
GraphSAGE vs MLP advantage statistically significant
Framework consumes real OSM data end-to-end for production cities
Fragility ensemble (HAZUS+GEM-like+Jaiswal-like) epistemic uncertainty propagated to results
PCMCI direct edge dGW → damage weak when controlling for soil + liq mediators (mediator-only structure)
Christchurch retrospective: predicted PGA p10-p90 encompasses observed 2010 and 2011 instrumental range without city-specific calibration

deprecated_claims: C05a R2 over-claim of statistically significant Mexico-City negative shift
